{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-35381", "cvss": {"score": 3.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 3.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-684", "name": "Incorrect Provision of Specified Functionality"}], "description": "`cut` routes `-z -d ''` through a special newline-delimiter path that ignores the `-s` only-delimited flag, emitting whole undelimited records (plus NUL) that should be suppressed. Pipelines relying on `cut -s` to drop undelimited records process data that should be filtered.
```
printf 'abc' | cut -z -d '' -s -f 1 | od -An -tx1   # GNU: no output ; uutils: 61 62 63 00
```

---
_Zellic private finding (zellic-ext/coreutils-private PR #102).
